title: Getting started with crawlee
text: crawlee mirrors architecture Crawlee pure R. crawler owns: request queue — deduplicating, resumable list URLs visit; one handlers — functions run fetched page; dataset — structured records handlers produce. build crawler crawler() configure cr_* verbs compose native pipe (|>).

title: Getting started with crawlee
code: resultado <- crawler("https://example.com") |>   cr_options(delay = 0.5, max_depth = 2) |>   cr_use_http() |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(       url    = ctx$request$url,       titulo = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("h1") |> rvest::html_text2()     ))     ctx$enqueue_links()   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()

title: Getting started with crawlee
text: Every handler receives context object, conventionally named ctx:

title: Getting started with crawlee
text: enqueue_links() accepts glob, include/exclude patterns same_domain flag (default), follow links care : Requests enqueued label routed matching handler registered cr_on_html(..., label = "article").

title: Getting started with crawlee
text: request queue deduplicates URLs normalised key (see cr_normalize_url()), page never fetched twice crawls deterministic. Persistent, resumable storage backends (DuckDB, Parquet) roadmap. ```

title: Crawling a website
text: crawler owns three things: request queue — deduplicating, resumable list URLs visit; one handlers — functions run fetched page; dataset — structured records handlers produce. build crawler crawler() configure cr_* verbs compose native pipe (|>), run cr_run().

title: Crawling a website
text: Fetch single page extract couple fields. handler receives context object (ctx) exposing parsed page action push_data().
code: result <- crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(       url   = ctx$request$url,       title = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("title") |> rvest::html_text2()     ))   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()  result

title: Crawling a website
text: Real crawls discover new URLs go. ctx$enqueue_links() extracts links current page adds queue; crawler keeps going queue drains. queue deduplicates normalised URL, page visited . enqueue_links() follows -domain links default, crawl wander across whole web.
code: crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))     ctx$enqueue_links() # follow every same-domain link   }) |>   cr_options(max_requests = 50) |>   cr_run()

title: Crawling a website
text: rarely want every link. enqueue_links() takes glob (shorthand include), include/exclude patterns same_domain flag; crawler enforces max_depth max_requests.
code: crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_options(max_depth = 3, max_requests = 200) |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url, depth = ctx$request$depth))     ctx$enqueue_links(       glob    = "*/catalogue/*", # only follow catalogue pages       exclude = "*/category/*"     )   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()

title: Crawling a website
text: sites kinds page — listings vs. detail pages, say. Give label enqueuing register handler label. Listing pages enqueue detail pages; detail pages extract data. request’s label always wins content-kind default, labelled routing cr_on_html()/cr_on_pdf() defaults compose cleanly.
code: books <- crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   # listing pages: enqueue book detail pages, labelled "book"   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/catalogue/*index.html", label = "book")     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/page-*.html") # pagination, default handler   }) |>   # detail pages   cr_on_html(label = "book", function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(       title = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("h1") |> rvest::html_text2(),       price = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element(".price_color") |> rvest::html_text2()     ))   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()  books

title: Crawling a website
text: site publishes sitemap.xml, can seed queue directly instead discovering links page page — cr_from_sitemap() handles sitemap indexes gzipped sitemaps, can filter glob <lastmod> date. companion cr_from_rss() RSS Atom feeds.
code: crawler() |>   cr_from_sitemap("https://books.toscrape.com/sitemap.xml", label = "book") |>   cr_on_html(label = "book", function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()

title: Crawling a website
text: page builds content JavaScript, plain HTTP backend sees empty shell. Switch headless-browser backend cr_use_browser() (requires package Chrome/Chromium install). Handlers unchanged; additionally get ctx$screenshot().
code: crawler("https://example.com") |>   cr_use_browser(wait_selector = ".content") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))     ctx$screenshot()   }) |>   cr_run()

title: Crawling a website
text: Politeness & speed — robots.txt respected default; cr_options(delay = ) rate-limits, cr_parallel() fetches concurrently. Documents — cr_on_pdf() extracts text PDFs; ctx$save_body() stores raw files key-value store. Reproducible, resumable runs — cr_persist(dir) checkpoints queue persists dataset, interrupted crawl continues left . RAG — cr_chunk(), cr_embed() cr_export() turn crawled text retrieval-ready table.

title: A RAG pipeline
code: pages <- crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_options(max_requests = 100) |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(       url   = ctx$request$url,       title = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("title") |> rvest::html_text2(),       text  = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("body") |> rvest::html_text2()     ))     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/catalogue/*")   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()

title: A RAG pipeline
text: cr_chunk() splits text overlapping windows. data frame, name text column; every column carried along per-chunk metadata (chunk keeps url title). Use = "word" size chunks words instead characters.
code: chunks <- cr_chunk(pages, text = text, size = 1000, overlap = 200, by = "char") chunks #> columns: doc_id, chunk_id, chunk, text, n_chars, url, title

title: A RAG pipeline
text: cr_embed() provider-agnostic: crawlee never calls embedding service . pass embed_fn, function maps character vector numeric matrix (one row per input) list numeric vectors. applied batches adds embedding list-column. quick local experiment can pass function — even trivial one:
code: # A real embedder typically calls an HTTP API (any provider) with httr2: embed_fn <- function(texts) {   # return a length(texts) x d numeric matrix   resp <- httr2::request("https://your-embeddings-endpoint/v1/embeddings") |>     httr2::req_auth_bearer_token(Sys.getenv("EMBEDDINGS_API_KEY")) |>     httr2::req_body_json(list(input = texts)) |>     httr2::req_perform()   do.call(rbind, lapply(httr2::resp_body_json(resp)$data, \(x) unlist(x$embedding))) }  embedded <- cr_embed(chunks, embed_fn, batch_size = 32) fake_embed <- function(x) matrix(nchar(x), nrow = length(x), ncol = 1) embedded <- cr_embed(chunks, fake_embed)

title: A RAG pipeline
text: cr_export() writes chunk table (embeddings) retrieval-friendly format. parquet jsonl preserve embedding vectors natively; csv duckdb serialise [...] string.
code: cr_export(embedded, "corpus.parquet", format = "parquet") cr_export(embedded, "corpus.jsonl", format = "jsonl") cr_export(embedded, "corpus.duckdb", format = "duckdb", table = "chunks")

title: A RAG pipeline
text: , load corpus.parquet vector store nearest-neighbour search R retrieve chunks prompt.
code: crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_options(max_requests = 100) |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(       url  = ctx$request$url,       text = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("body") |> rvest::html_text2()     ))     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/catalogue/*")   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect() |>   cr_chunk(text = text, size = 1000, overlap = 200) |>   cr_embed(embed_fn) |>   cr_export("corpus.parquet", format = "parquet")

title: Scaling and politeness
text: default crawlee conservative respectful: robots.txt honoured (respect_robots = TRUE): disallowed URLs skipped, Crawl-delay directive applied. set descriptive user_agent site owners can identify crawler; delay adds pause requests; max_requests max_depth bound crawl; failed requests retried (max_retries) backoff.
code: crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_options(     user_agent = "my-research-bot (you@example.com)",     delay = 0.5, # seconds between requests     max_requests = 500,     max_depth = 4,     respect_robots = TRUE   ) |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) ctx$enqueue_links()) |>   cr_run()

title: Scaling and politeness
text: default engine sequential. higher throughput three concurrent engines; keep handlers running sequentially R (dataset queue never touched concurrently) — network /O runs parallel.

title: Scaling and politeness
text: Drains queue batches whose network requests run together.

title: Scaling and politeness
text: Like cr_parallel(), batch size adapts run time (additive-increase clean batches, halving back-pressure HTTP 429/503 transport failures), staying within [min, max].
code: crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_autoscale(min = 2, max = 16) |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) ctx$enqueue_links()) |>   cr_run()

title: Scaling and politeness
text: Keeps concurrency requests flight times: moment one finishes, handler runs next request pulled . avoids batch engines’ “wait slowest request batch” stall shines response latency varies lot.

title: Scaling and politeness
text: Concurrency politeness pull opposite directions. batch engines apply delay / Crawl-delay batches; streaming engine treats concurrency throttle enforce per-request pacing. strict rate limits, prefer batch engine delay.

title: Scaling and politeness
text: engine composes cr_persist() resumable, checkpointed runs:
code: crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_autoscale(min = 2, max = 16) |>   cr_persist("runs/books", dataset = "duckdb") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) ctx$enqueue_links()) |>   cr_run()

title: Storage and resumable runs
text: Handlers call ctx$push_data() append records; cr_collect() returns one tibble. default dataset lives memory. larger longer crawls, choose persistent backend cr_dataset(): "jsonl" — append-, schema-flexible, one JSON object per line; "duckdb" — appended DuckDB table, ready SQL. persistent backends resume existing file: re-opening path keeps rows already .
code: result <- crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect() crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_dataset(backend = "duckdb", path = "books.duckdb") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))) |>   cr_run()

title: Storage and resumable runs
text: Use key-value store raw, non-tabular content — PDFs, images, page snapshots. ctx$save_body() writes current response , cr_store() sets directory.
code: crawler("https://example.com/report.pdf") |>   cr_store("downloads") |>   cr_on_pdf(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url, pages = length(ctx$pdf_text())))     ctx$save_body(ext = "pdf") # -> downloads/<sanitised-url>.pdf   }) |>   cr_run()

title: Storage and resumable runs
text: request queue deduplicates normalised key (see cr_normalize_url()), URL fetched crawl deterministic. can also persist state — pending requests, seen keys, handled count — makes crawl resumable.

title: Storage and resumable runs
text: cr_persist(dir) wires everything run directory: queue checkpointed queue.rds run; dataset uses persistent backend (dataset.jsonl dataset.duckdb); ctx$save_body() writes kv/; manifest (manifest.rds / manifest.json) records start URLs, options snapshot run statistics.
code: crawl <- crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_persist("runs/books", dataset = "duckdb") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/catalogue/*")   }) |>   cr_run()  data <- cr_collect(crawl) cr_close(crawl) # release the DuckDB connection

title: Storage and resumable runs
text: run interrupted, run exact pipeline . state already exists runs/books, cr_persist() restores crawl continues left — already-fetched URLs skipped. DuckDB backend, call cr_collect() cr_close() — closing releases connection.
code: # Same code as above: it resumes instead of starting over. crawler("https://books.toscrape.com/") |>   cr_persist("runs/books", dataset = "duckdb") |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/catalogue/*")   }) |>   cr_run()

what: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling
title: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling
text: tidy R interface reproducible web crawling — inspired architecture Crawlee, implemented pure R. crawlee brings unified-crawler idea R: deduplicating, resumable request queue, content-type aware handlers, structured storage rich console logging via cli. can crawl HTML pages, sitemaps, RSS Atom feeds PDF documents — reproducibility first-class concern. built entirely R web-scraping ecosystem (httr2, rvest, xml2, chromote) — Node.js runtime required.

title: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling
A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling
code: library(crawlee)  resultado <- crawler("https://example.com") |>   cr_options(delay = 0.5, max_depth = 2, respect_robots = TRUE) |>   cr_use_http() |>   cr_on_html(function(ctx) {     ctx$push_data(list(       url    = ctx$request$url,       titulo = ctx$page |> rvest::html_element("h1") |> rvest::html_text2()     ))     ctx$enqueue_links(glob = "*/blog/*")   }) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect()  resultado #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   url                 titulo #>   <chr>               <chr> #> 1 https://example.com Example Domain

title: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling
text: Reproducibility first — deduplicating, resumable request queue; runs meant deterministic re-runnable. heavy mandatory dependencies — DuckDB, chromote pdftools optional (Suggests), loaded used. Tidy & predictable — cr_* verbs compose native pipe always return tibbles. polite web citizen — rate limiting robots.txt awareness default.

title: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling
text: Next: persistent/resumable dataset backends (DuckDB, Parquet) parallel/autoscaled fetching.

title: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling

title: Crawler — Crawler-class
text: stateful object centre crawlee. holds request queue, dataset, registered handlers run configuration. rarely create one Crawler$new() directly; use crawler() cr_* verbs, return crawler invisibly compose native pipe (|>).

title: Crawler — Crawler-class
text: options Named list run options. queue RequestQueue. dataset Dataset. handlers Named list label-specific handlers. defaults Named list default handlers content kind (html, pdf, ). kv Lazily-created KeyValueStore binary content. mode Fetch mode, "http" (default) "browser". stats Named list run statistics.

title: Dataset — Dataset
text: append-structured store records produced handlers via ctx$push_data(). Three backends available: "memory" (default): records accumulate memory. "jsonl": record appended line JSON file — schema-flexible, append-resumable across runs. "duckdb": records appended table DuckDB database, ready SQL analysis. Collect everything single tibble cr_collect().

title: Dataset — Dataset
text: backend Name storage backend. path Path persistent backends.

title: Request queue — RequestQueue
text: deduplicating, FIFO--priority request queue, -memory engine behind every crawler(). Requests keyed normalised unique_key (see cr_normalize_url()) URL never enqueued twice. queue tracks requests handled, makes crawl resumable: given path, state (pending requests, seen keys, handled count) can saved restored disk — see cr_persist(). class exported mainly advanced use introspection; users interact indirectly cr_* verbs.

title: Enable autoscaled parallel fetching — cr_autoscale
text: Like cr_parallel(), batch concurrency adapts run time, rough equivalent Crawlee's autoscaled pool. batch engine adjusts concurrency additive-increase / multiplicative-decrease rule: grows one batch clean, halves back-pressure (transport failure HTTP 429/500/502/503/504), staying within [min, max].

title: Enable autoscaled parallel fetching — cr_autoscale
text: crawler Crawler. min, max Concurrency bounds. crawl starts min. max_active Maximum simultaneously-active connections (defaults current concurrency).

title: Enable autoscaled parallel fetching — cr_autoscale
text: crawler, invisibly.

what: Chunk text for retrieval-augmented generation — cr_chunk
title: Chunk text for retrieval-augmented generation — cr_chunk
text: Splits documents overlapping chunks suitable embedding retrieval. Works character vector (one element per document) data frame, case chunked column replaced text columns carried along per-chunk metadata.

title: Chunk text for retrieval-augmented generation — cr_chunk
code: cr_chunk(   data,   text = NULL,   size = 1000L,   overlap = 200L,   by = c("char", "word") )

title: Chunk text for retrieval-augmented generation — cr_chunk
text: data character vector data frame (e.g. result cr_collect()). text data data frame, (unquoted) column holding text chunk. size Target chunk size, characters (= "char") words (= "word"). overlap Overlap consecutive chunks, unit size. Must smaller size. "char" (default) "word".

title: Chunk text for retrieval-augmented generation — cr_chunk
text: tibble columns doc_id, chunk_id (within document), chunk (global index), text, n_chars, plus carried metadata.

title: Chunk text for retrieval-augmented generation — cr_chunk
code: cr_chunk(c("um texto longo ...", "outro documento ..."),          size = 10, overlap = 2, by = "word") #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   doc_id chunk_id text                n_chars chunk #>    <int>    <int> <chr>                 <int> <int> #> 1      1        1 um texto longo ...       18     1 #> 2      2        1 outro documento ...      19     2

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — cr_embed
text: Adds embedding list-column applying user-supplied, provider- agnostic embedding function batches. crawlee never calls external service : pass embed_fn, receives character vector returns either numeric matrix (one row per input) list numeric vectors. keeps free use provider local model.

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — cr_embed
text: data data frame text column (e.g. cr_chunk()). embed_fn function mapping character vector numeric matrix (rows = inputs) list numeric vectors. text_col Name text column. Defaults "text". batch_size Number texts per call embed_fn.

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — cr_embed
text: data added embedding list-column.

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — cr_embed
code: chunks <- cr_chunk(c("a b c d", "e f g h"), size = 2, overlap = 0, by = "word") fake_embed <- function(x) matrix(nchar(x), nrow = length(x), ncol = 1) cr_embed(chunks, fake_embed) #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   doc_id chunk_id text  n_chars chunk embedding #>    <int>    <int> <chr>   <int> <int> <list>    #> 1      1        1 a b         3     1 <dbl [1]> #> 2      1        2 c d         3     2 <dbl [1]> #> 3      2        1 e f         3     3 <dbl [1]> #> 4      2        2 g h         3     4 <dbl [1]>

code: cr_from_rss(   crawler,   url,   label = NULL,   include = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   max = Inf )

title: Discover URLs from an RSS or Atom feed — cr_from_rss
text: crawler Crawler. url URL RSS Atom feed. label Optional handler label routing enqueued URLs. include, exclude Optional glob patterns (see cr_on_html()). max Maximum number items enqueue.

text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Discover URLs from an RSS or Atom feed — cr_from_rss
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ crawler() |>   cr_on_html(\(ctx) ctx$push_data(list(     url = ctx$request$url, title = ctx$request$user_data$title   ))) |>   cr_from_rss("https://example.com/feed.xml") } # }

what: Discover URLs from a sitemap — cr_from_sitemap
title: Discover URLs from a sitemap — cr_from_sitemap
text: Fetches sitemap (sitemap index, recursively) enqueues page URLs lists. Supports gzipped sitemaps, glob filtering since filter <lastmod> incremental re-crawls large sites publish dated sitemaps.

title: Discover URLs from a sitemap — cr_from_sitemap
code: cr_from_sitemap(   crawler,   url,   label = NULL,   include = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   since = NULL,   max = Inf,   max_levels = 3L )

title: Discover URLs from a sitemap — cr_from_sitemap
text: crawler Crawler. url URL sitemap.xml sitemap index. label Optional handler label routing enqueued URLs. include, exclude Optional glob patterns (see cr_on_html()). since Optional date (YYYY-MM-DD string); URLs whose <lastmod> date enqueued (URLs without lastmod kept). max Maximum number URLs enqueue. max_levels Maximum recursion depth nested sitemap indexes.

title: Discover URLs from a sitemap — cr_from_sitemap
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Discover URLs from a sitemap — cr_from_sitemap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ crawler() |>   cr_on_html(\(ctx) ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))) |>   cr_from_sitemap("https://example.com/sitemap.xml", since = "2026-01-01") } # }

title: Normalise a URL into a canonical form — cr_normalize_url
text: Produces canonical representation URL used deduplication key (unique_key) request. Normalisation lower-cases scheme host, removes trailing slash path, drops default ports sorts query parameters semantically identical URLs collapse key.

Register an HTML handler — cr_on_html
text: Registers function called successfully fetched page whose request carries given label (pages label = NULL). handler receives context object exposing parsed page actions push_data() enqueue_links().

title: Register an HTML handler — cr_on_html
text: crawler Crawler. handler function one argument (context). See Context. label Optional handler label. Requests enqueued label routed ; NULL registers default handler.

title: Register an HTML handler — cr_on_html
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Register an HTML handler — cr_on_html
text: ctx passed handler contains: request request list (url, label, depth, ...). response httr2 response object. page parsed page (xml_document) NULL. push_data(data) Append record dataset. enqueue_links(...) Discover enqueue links page. log Logging functions (info, success, warn, error).

title: Register a PDF handler — cr_on_pdf
text: Registers handler invoked responses classified PDF — Content-Type (application/pdf) .pdf URL. handler context adds PDF-specific helpers top usual ones.

title: Register a PDF handler — cr_on_pdf
text: crawler Crawler. handler function one argument (context). See Context. label Optional handler label; NULL registers default PDF handler.

title: Register a PDF handler — cr_on_pdf
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Register a PDF handler — cr_on_pdf
text: Requests carrying explicit label always routed handler registered label (regardless content kind); label = NULL registers default PDF handler.

title: Register a PDF handler — cr_on_pdf
text: addition elements documented cr_on_html(), PDF handler's context provides: kind "pdf". pdf_text() Extract text per page (requires pdftools package), returning character vector. body_raw() raw PDF bytes. save_body(key, ext) Persist PDF KeyValueStore.

title: Register a PDF handler — cr_on_pdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ crawler("https://example.com/report.pdf") |>   cr_on_pdf(function(ctx) {     text <- ctx$pdf_text()     ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url, n_pages = length(text)))     ctx$save_body(ext = "pdf")   }) |>   cr_run() } # }

title: Set crawler options — cr_options
text: crawler Crawler. ... Named options override. Recognised options: concurrency, max_requests, max_depth, delay (seconds requests), timeout, max_retries, user_agent, respect_robots, same_domain, log_level ("debug", "info", "warn", "error", "").

title: Set crawler options — cr_options
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Enable parallel (concurrent) fetching — cr_parallel
text: Switches HTTP engine fetch requests concurrent batches via httr2::req_perform_parallel(), rough equivalent Crawlee's autoscaled pool. Network /O runs concurrently handlers still run sequentially R, shared-state hazard. robots.txt, retries, max_requests/max_depth queue checkpointing still apply.

title: Enable parallel (concurrent) fetching — cr_parallel
text: crawler Crawler. concurrency Number requests per batch. max_active Maximum simultaneously-active connections (defaults concurrency).

title: Enable parallel (concurrent) fetching — cr_parallel
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Enable parallel (concurrent) fetching — cr_parallel
text: Parallel mode applies HTTP backend ; browser backend always runs sequentially. delay Crawl-delay applied batches.

what: Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
title: Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
text: Wires crawler directory disk crawl reproducible resumable. persists:

title: Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
text: crawler Crawler. dir Run directory (created needed). dataset Dataset backend use: "jsonl" (default), "duckdb" "memory" (persisted).

title: Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
text: request queue state (queue.rds) — pending requests, seen keys handled count, checkpointed cr_run(); dataset, via persistent Dataset backend (dataset.jsonl dataset.duckdb); binary content saved ctx$save_body() (kv/); run manifest (manifest.rds, plus manifest.json jsonlite available). queue state already exists dir, crawl resumes: saved pending/seen/handled state restored, cr_run() continues left already-fetched URLs fetched . Call cr_persist() cr_run(). "duckdb" backend, collect results cr_collect() cr_close().

title: Persist a crawl to a run directory (and resume it) — cr_persist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ crawler("https://example.com") |>   cr_persist("runs/example", dataset = "duckdb") |>   cr_on_html(\(ctx) ctx$push_data(list(url = ctx$request$url))) |>   cr_run() |>   cr_collect() # Re-running the same pipeline resumes from runs/example. } # }

text: Drains request queue, fetching request, dispatching matching handler collecting pushed records, queue empty max_requests limit reached.

title: Enable the streaming scheduler — cr_stream
text: continuous-pool alternative cr_parallel()'s synchronous batches. streaming engine keeps concurrency requests flight times (via async promises, httr2::req_perform_promise()): moment one request finishes, handler runs next request pulled queue refill slot. heterogeneous response latency avoids "wait slowest batch" stall improves throughput.

title: Enable the streaming scheduler — cr_stream
text: crawler Crawler. concurrency Number requests keep flight.

title: Enable the streaming scheduler — cr_stream
text: crawler, invisibly.

title: Enable the streaming scheduler — cr_stream
text: Requires promises later packages, HTTP backend. Concurrency throttle; delay / robots.txt Crawl-delay pacing enforced streaming mode (use cr_parallel() strict pacing). robots.txt allow/deny rules still respected.

what: crawlee: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling — crawlee-package
title: crawlee: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling — crawlee-package
text: tidy, pipe-friendly toolkit reproducible web crawling structured data collection, inspired architecture 'Crawlee' library. Provides unified crawler deduplicating, resumable request queue, content-type aware handlers, structured storage backends rich console logging via 'cli'. Supports crawling HTML pages, sitemaps, RSS Atom feeds PDF documents, optional headless-browser rendering helpers retrieval-augmented generation.

title: crawlee: A Tidy R Interface for Reproducible Web Crawling — crawlee-package
text: Maintainer: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Authors: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Marcos Wasilew marcos.wasilew@gmail.com Hugo Vasconcelos hugo.vasconcelos@ufpe.br Carlos Amorin carlos.agaf@ufpe.br Diogo Bezerra diogo.bezerra@ufpe.br Tiago Pereira tiago.pereira@tesouro.gov.br Fernando Barbalho fbarbalho@gmail.com

text: Constructs new Crawler seeded start_urls. result designed piped cr_* configuration verbs finally cr_run().

text: start_urls Character vector seed URLs enqueue depth 0. ... Options forwarded cr_options() (e.g. max_requests, delay, log_level).

title: Create a crawler — crawler
text: Crawler object.

title: Create a crawler — crawler
code: cr <- crawler("https://example.com", max_requests = 10) cr #> <Crawler> ("http" mode) #> • pending requests: 1 #> • handled: 0 - records: 0 #> • handlers: 0 labelled - defaults: none

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: First scaffold package. Crawlee-inspired, native-R architecture.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: cr_autoscale(min, max) adapts parallel batch concurrency run time (Crawlee autoscaled-pool style): additive-increase clean batches, multiplicative-decrease back-pressure (transport failure HTTP 429/500/502/503/504), clamped [min, max]. cr_stream(concurrency) adds continuous-pool scheduler (via httr2::req_perform_promise() + /): keeps concurrency requests flight times, dispatching refilling finishes — avoiding batch engine’s “wait slowest” stall.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: cr_parallel(concurrency) enables concurrent fetching HTTP backend (Crawlee’s autoscaled-pool equivalent): queue drained batches whose network /O runs concurrently via httr2::req_perform_parallel(), handlers still run sequentially R (shared-state hazard). robots.txt, retries, depth/request limits queue checkpointing still apply; delay/Crawl-delay applied batches. engine refactored shared dispatch/error steps used sequential parallel loops.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: cr_persist() ties crawl run directory: request queue checkpointed (queue.rds) run restored next run, crawl resumes left without re-fetching seen URLs. Persistent [Dataset] backends: cr_dataset(backend = "jsonl") (append-, schema-flexible) "duckdb" (SQL-ready). RequestQueue gained save()/restore()/set_path(). reproducibility manifest (manifest.rds / manifest.json) records start URLs, options snapshot run stats. cr_close() releases browser session DuckDB connection.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: cr_chunk() splits text (character vector data-frame column) overlapping chunks, character word, carrying metadata per chunk. cr_embed() attaches embedding list-column via user-supplied, provider-agnostic embedding function, applied batches. crawlee never calls external service . cr_export() writes chunks (embeddings) Parquet, JSONL, CSV DuckDB retrieval.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: cr_use_browser() renders JavaScript-heavy pages headless Chrome/Chromium via , wait wait_selector controls. Handlers unchanged (ctx$page, enqueue_links()); context gains ctx$screenshot(), saved [KeyValueStore]. Fetch backends now unified behind normalised internal fetched object, handlers behave identically regardless HTTP vs browser.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Content-type aware dispatch: response classified (html, pdf, ) routed matching default handler; explicit request labels still take precedence. cr_on_pdf() registers PDF handler. context adds pdf_text() (per-page text via ), body_raw()/body_string() save_body(). KeyValueStore plus cr_store() ctx$save_body(): persist raw responses (PDFs, images, snapshots) disk alongside structured dataset.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: cr_from_sitemap() enqueues URLs sitemap.xml, recursing sitemap indexes, transparently handling gzipped sitemaps, glob filters since filter <lastmod> incremental crawls. cr_from_rss() enqueues items RSS Atom feeds, carrying item title date request’s user_data. robots.txt now enforced respect_robots = TRUE (default): native parser/matcher (User-agent grouping, */$ patterns, longest-match Allow override, Crawl-delay), cached per host. Disallowed URLs skipped reported; Crawl-delay honoured.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: crawler() builds stateful, pipe-friendly crawler. RequestQueue: deduplicating (normalised unique_key), FIFO, resumable request queue retry rescheduling. cr_options() configures concurrency, depth, delay, retries, user agent log verbosity. cr_use_http() HTTP fetch backend (httr2); cr_use_browser() reserved. cr_on_html() registers content handlers; handler context exposes push_data() enqueue_links() (glob/include/exclude -domain filtering). Dataset append-store; cr_run() drives crawl cr_collect() returns tibble. Rich console logging via cli.

title: crawlee 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Autoscaling within streaming scheduler (currently fixed concurrency); per-host rate-limit pacing streaming mode.
